tiles.text: You only exist because of me

groups.name: Westworld Universe
groups.explanation: The Man in Black (character), Dolores (character), Maeve Millay (character), and 'You only exist because of me' (quote spoken in Westworld) are all from HBO's Westworld.

groups.name: Characters Who Were Secretly Much Older Than They Appeared
groups.explanation: Melisandre (Game of Thrones) was revealed to be centuries old beneath her illusion; Saul Tigh (Battlestar Galactica) was revealed to be an ancient Cylon who had lived thousands of years without knowing it; Elim Garak (DS9) was much older than his demeanor suggested and concealed a full hidden life; Caprica City is the ancestral home city tied to the Cylons' ancient origins — all connect to characters or places whose true age/history was concealed and then shockingly revealed.

groups.name: Actors Who Played a Character With a Double Identity Hidden From Their Closest Ally for Multiple Seasons
groups.explanation: Penn Badgley (Joe in You — hid his true murderous identity from love interests for seasons); Carice van Houten (Melisandre in Game of Thrones — hid her true ancient form from all allies for years); Evan Rachel Wood (Dolores in Westworld — hid that she had absorbed other identities and was orchestrating events); Henry Cavill (Geralt in The Witcher — his true nature and timeline were hidden from viewers and characters for the whole first season). Each actor played a character whose double identity was concealed from their closest ally across multiple seasons.

groups.name: TV Characters Who Were Given a Backstory Reveal Only in the Final Season — But Fans Had Already Correctly Theorized It Years Earlier
groups.explanation: Tatiana Maslany's Sarah Manning (Orphan Black) — fans theorized the full clone conspiracy scope before it was confirmed in the final season; Katee Sackhoff's Starbuck (BSG) — fans theorized she was an angel/harbinger of death from early on, confirmed in the finale; Mozzie (White Collar) — fans theorized for years his real name and identity were a spy cover, confirmed very late; Shepherd Book (Firefly/Serenity) — fans widely theorized his Alliance past years before Serenity confirmed it. These are characters whose long-theorized secret backstory was only officially confirmed in the show's final season or film.
